description_long: This is a classic steam locomotive with a prominent cylindrical boiler at the front, followed by a coal tender attached at the rear. The locomotive features large driving wheels, a smokestack, and a series of mechanical linkages along its side, characteristic of early to mid-20th-century steam engines. The tender is boxy and rides on two sets of wheels, designed to carry coal and water for the engine. There are visible ladders and steps for crew access, and the overall structure is robust, built primarily from metal. The exterior is mostly dark, with subtle highlights indicating metallic surfaces and some red accents near the front.
description: A dark, robust steam locomotive with a cylindrical boiler, large wheels, and an attached coal tender, featuring metallic surfaces and mechanical details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow face of the locomotive, with the smokestack and front coupler visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the locomotive and tender, highlighting the large wheels, mechanical rods, and ladder.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the tender, showing its boxy shape and rear wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the other side of the locomotive and tender with mechanical details.
